paper_title: Variations on Sidorenko's conjecture in tournaments
summary: The conjecture remains open in full generality. The source paper (2402.08418) already confirms it for trees with at most one vertex of even degree. A follow-up paper (arXiv:2512.11222, He–Mani–Nie–Tung–Wei, 2025) proves the conjecture for all caterpillars (Theorem 1.4) and reduces the full conjecture to a restricted class: trees that are not caterpillars with at least 2 vertices of even degree and no isomorphic pair (their Conjecture 1.5). Specific open cases such as the '2-3-4 tree' are identified as Problem 1.6.

since_posted.title: New Sidorenko-type inequalities in tournaments
since_posted.authors: Xiaoyu He, Nitya Mani, Jiaxi Nie, Nathan Tung, Fan Wei
since_posted.claim: Proves every caterpillar has a tournament anti-Sidorenko orientation (Theorem 1.4) and reduces Conjecture 1.12 to the subclass of non-caterpillar trees with at least 2 vertices of even degree and no isomorphic pair (Conjecture 1.5); the full conjecture remains open.

internal_refs_verified.note: Paper is 'Induced subgraph density. VI. Bounded VC-dimension' by Nguyen, Scott, Seymour — concerns the Erdos-Hajnal conjecture for two-colourable tournaments, not Sidorenko's conjecture in tournaments. The internal reference attribution is a false positive; this paper does not address Conjecture 1.12 from 2402.08418.

Paper is 'Induced subgraph density. VII. The five-vertex path' by Nguyen, Scott, Seymour — proves the Erdos-Hajnal conjecture for P_5, unrelated to tournament anti-Sidorenko orientation of trees. The internal reference attribution is a false positive.

Sidorenko conjecture tournaments trees Fox Himwich Mani Zhou anti-Sidorenko orientation
notes: The two internal references (2312.15572 and 2312.15333) are false positives from fuzzy matching — both are about Erdos-Hajnal / induced subgraph density and have no bearing on Conjecture 1.12. The only confirmed follow-up is arXiv:2512.11222 (verified via WebFetch), which gives substantial partial progress.
